skill.name: supabase
skill.description: Guides Supabase development with current documentation checks, security-focused RLS guidance, CLI and MCP workflows, and safe migration practices.
skill.summary: Use current Supabase guidance for auth, RLS, CLI, MCP, storage, and schema work.
skill.author: supabase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many severe patterns, but review found no malicious code, prompt injection, or credential exfiltration. Most detections are false positives from Markdown examples, documentation URLs, and Supabase security terminology. The skill still carries medium operational risk because it instructs agents to run Supabase CLI, MCP, SQL, migration, and network checks that can affect real projects.

medium_findings.title: Database-Mutating Command Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends direct SQL execution and Supabase migration commands through MCP or the CLI. This is legitimate for a Supabase assistant, but misuse could alter schemas, policies, or data in a real project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly instruct use of execute_sql, supabase db query, advisors, db pull, and migration list. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the commands match the skill purpose.

medium_findings.title: External Network and MCP Connectivity Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to consult Supabase URLs, configure the Supabase MCP endpoint, and run a curl health check. The endpoints are legitimate Supabase resources, but the workflow depends on network access and OAuth setup.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs and curl command are present in the reviewed file and point to Supabase-controlled resources. The concern is bounded network interaction, not evidence of exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick alerts are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and fenced command examples. No executable script, hook, or automatic command runner was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain Markdown instructions and command examples only. I found no runtime code path that executes these strings automatically.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Alerts Are Relative Documentation Paths
low_findings.description: The path traversal alert is a false positive from a relative Markdown link to the local feedback template. The .mcp.json mention is configuration guidance and does not read or write files by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ../ sequence appears inside a repository-relative documentation link. The MCP configuration reference is descriptive text, not file access code.

low_findings.title: Sensitive and Weak Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM database and weak cryptographic algorithm alerts are not supported by the reviewed text. The cited locations discuss Supabase authorization, RLS, documentation, MCP, and feedback templates.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not mention Windows SAM, weak algorithms, or cryptographic implementation choices. They contain examples, URLs, and Supabase workflow instructions.

low_findings.title: User-Approved Feedback Submission
low_findings.description: The feedback workflow can send issue content to GitHub, but it explicitly instructs the agent to ask user permission first. This lowers the risk, though users should avoid including secrets in feedback issues.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The review found clear permission language before issue submission and a GitHub issue URL fallback. The remaining concern is accidental disclosure in user-provided feedback text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Manual Supabase CLI and SQL Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes command guidance for Supabase CLI and SQL execution. These commands are user-directed and legitimate, but they can change database state if applied to the wrong project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Command examples and database change workflows are explicit in the skill. They are not hidden or automatic, so the pattern is operationally dangerous rather than malicious.

content.value_statement: Supabase projects can fail when auth, RLS, CLI, or MCP details are outdated. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through current Supabase workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to verify Supabase behavior against current documentation before implementation.
Provides security checks for RLS, JWT claims, service keys, views, storage, and privileged functions.
Explains how to discover Supabase CLI commands and version-specific capabilities.
Describes Supabase MCP setup, connectivity checks, OAuth authentication, and documentation search order.
Recommends migration workflows using advisors, schema diffing, and local verification.
limitations: It does not include executable automation or Supabase API clients.
It depends on external Supabase documentation, CLI, or MCP tools for current details.
It cannot verify project safety without access to the target Supabase environment.
Database commands still require careful human review before production use.

use_cases.title: Secure RLS Policy Review
use_cases.description: Review policies, JWT claims, and exposed schemas before shipping user-facing data access.

use_cases.title: Supabase CLI Troubleshooting
use_cases.description: Check CLI versions, discover command flags, and choose fallbacks when commands are unavailable.

use_cases.title: MCP-Assisted Schema Work
use_cases.description: Use Supabase MCP tools and local migrations to iterate on database changes with verification.

prompt_templates.title: Check My RLS Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Supabase table access plan. Identify RLS policies, JWT claim risks, and missing verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot Supabase CLI
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me diagnose this Supabase CLI error. Check the command version, expected flags, and safe fallback options.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Migration Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a safe Supabase schema migration for this change. Include advisor checks, diff generation, and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Auth and Storage Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Supabase Auth, Storage, and Postgres design. Focus on session freshness, service keys, views, functions, and upsert permissions.

output_examples.input: Review a proposed RLS policy that uses user-editable metadata for role checks.
output_examples.output: The review flags user-editable metadata as unsafe for authorization and recommends app metadata with token freshness checks.

output_examples.input: Diagnose why a Supabase storage upsert fails while initial uploads work.
output_examples.output: The answer explains that upsert needs insert, select, and update permissions, then lists verification steps.

output_examples.input: Prepare a local schema migration after testing direct SQL changes.
output_examples.output: The response outlines advisor checks, security checklist review, migration generation, and local migration listing.

best_practices: Verify Supabase behavior against current docs before implementing feature-specific guidance.
Run security advisor checks before generating or committing migration files.
Keep service keys and secret keys out of public clients and browser-exposed variables.
anti_patterns: Do not use user metadata claims for authorization decisions.
Do not put security definer functions in exposed schemas.
Do not run direct SQL against production without reviewing project targeting and rollback plans.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Supabase commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for agents. Users still control tool access and command execution.

faq.question: Why does the audit mark medium risk?
faq.answer: The skill can guide database, CLI, MCP, and network actions. These are legitimate but can affect real Supabase projects.

faq.question: Can it help with Row Level Security?
faq.answer: Yes. It highlights RLS defaults, JWT claim risks, view behavior, update policy requirements, and storage permissions.

faq.question: Does it require Supabase MCP?
faq.answer: No. MCP is preferred for some workflows, but the skill also references CLI and documentation-based fallbacks.

faq.question: Can it replace Supabase documentation?
faq.answer: No. The skill tells agents to check current Supabase documentation because product behavior changes over time.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production database changes?
faq.answer: It can support planning and review, but production changes need human approval, backups, and environment checks.
